state.next-action: 2026-04-18 sweep: site lane fully drained (45-06/09/10/11/12, 45-19/20, 44-16 — commit 99eb18e). Cancelled per user direction (twice): 42.2-05 (findings = docs material, `/findings` route does not exist on marketing site post-phase-46) and 42.3-13 (noise panel — cancelled outright). Verified-done: 44-38 (npx → GitHub Releases tarball) — `hooks/dist/gad-check-update.js` already polls `repos/${repo}/releases/latest` and `workflows/update.md` already uses `gh release download` + `tar -xzf` + `bin/install.js`; npx is gone everywhere. Caught + patched the adjacent regression in the same pass: v1.35.0 release ships only Bun binaries (no `get-anything-done-*.tgz`) so the tarball-download flow that 44-38 relies on would break for any tag pushed today. Restored: `.github/workflows/release-binaries.yml` publish job now `npm pack`s into `dist/release-upload` + verifies the tgz is present before upload; `scripts/publish-release.mjs` restored (`isReleaseArtifactName` accepts the tgz; `ensureReleaseTarball()` actually runs `npm pack`; `getArtifacts()` asserts it). Two regression-guard tests added to `tests/release-deprecation.test.cjs` (4 pass). v1.35.0 itself still missing the asset — captured in `.planning/notes/2026-04-18-v1.35.0-missing-npm-tarball.md` with operator recovery steps. Closed 63-health-cli in the same pass: `gad health disk|prune|caches` subtree wired into `bin/gad.cjs`; on-disk worktree gap closed via new `listAllWorktrees`/`discoverOnDiskAgentWorktrees` helpers (merges git-registered + `.claude|.codex|.cursor/worktrees/agent[-_]*`); 7-test suite (`tests/health-cli.test.cjs`) drives the CLI end-to-end against a scratch repo. Acceptance hit: disk ran in 2.0s with bounded walks; prune surfaces unregistered agent dirs; worktree list now flags them. Other follow-up still open: `.planning/notes/2026-04-18-findings-belong-in-docs-site.md` (mirror evals/FINDINGS-*.md into apps/portfolio docs). Next: pick from lane queue (likely the v1.35.0 tarball recovery — operator action — or a new framework slice).
